title: Michael B. Jordan Wins Best Actor Oscar for Sinners - Variety
text: Michael B. Jordan secured his first Oscar victory, winning Best Actor for his dual role as twin brothers Smoke and Stack in the supernatural thriller Sinners. This achievement makes him the sixth Black man to win in the lead actor category. During his acceptance speech, Jordan expressed gratitude to those who paved the way before him. He stated: I stand here because of the people that came before me, acknowledging Sidney Poitier, Denzel Washington, Jamie Foxx, Forest Whitaker, and Will Smith, as well as Halle Berry. Jordan also thanked his family members present at the ceremony, saying: Man, God is good. God is good, and notably mentioned that his father traveled from Ghana to attend the event. The actor praised director Ryan Coogler, with whom he has collaborated on five films including the Black Panther movies and Creed. Jordan called Coogler an amazing person and expressed honor at their partnership. Sinners, set in the 1930s South, became a box office phenomenon, earning $370 million globally. The film received 16 Oscar nominations and won four awards during the ceremony.
